title: Improved Bounds for the Freiman-Ruzsa Theorem

thm:1.1.summary: Main theorem: For any $\varepsilon>0$, $A$ with $|A+A| \le K|A|$ can be covered by $\exp(C_\varepsilon \log(2K)^{1+\varepsilon})$ translates of a convex coset progression with dimension and size bounds of the same form. Improves Sanders-Konyagin ($\varepsilon>2$) to any $\varepsilon>0$.

thm:1.3.summary: Covering by translates of a subspace ($\mathbb{F}_p^n$ case).

thm:1.4.summary: Alternative formulation of the main result.

conjecture:1.5.title: Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa Conjecture
conjecture:1.5.summary: The PFR conjecture: bounds should hold with $\varepsilon=0$. This paper approaches but does not reach it.

thm:1.8.summary: Variant formulation of the main result.

def:2.4.title: Entropic Ruzsa Distance
def:2.4.summary: Defines $d[X;Y] = H(X-Y) - H(X)/2 - H(Y)/2$, central to the entropy method.

def:2.6.title: Convex Coset Progression
def:2.6.summary: Defines the structured objects used in covering theorems.

def:2.8.title: Polynomial Growth
def:2.8.summary: Definition of polynomial growth for random variables.

thm:2.9.title: GGMT, Propositions 2.1 and 2.2
thm:2.9.summary: Cites GGMT's $\tau$-functional framework for PFR over $\mathbb{F}_2^n$.

def:2.10.title: $\tau$ functional
def:2.10.summary: Defines $\tau$-functional $\tau[X;Y] = d[X;Y] + \eta\, d[X;X] + \eta\, d[Y;Y]$, adapted from GGMT.

prop:3.1.summary: Obtaining a random variable with polynomial growth — first stage of the proof.

prop:4.1.summary: Structure from polynomial growth — second stage using Fourier analysis.

prop:4.2.summary: Bohr set structure from polynomial growth.

lem:A.9.title: Fibring Lemma
lem:A.9.summary: Fibring inequality for entropy — key tool from GGMT/GMT.

edges.note: The minimizer analysis is stated in terms of the $\tau$-functional definition.
The first stage uses the $\tau$-minimizer properties.
The main theorem rests on the first stage (obtaining polynomial growth).
The main theorem rests on the second stage (Fourier structure from polynomial growth).
The first stage uses the fibring lemma in its entropy arguments.
The second stage uses the weak Bogolyubov lemma to obtain Bohr-set structure.
The Bohr-set result uses Chang's lemma for spectrum analysis.
The $\tau$-functional definition is adapted from the GGMT framework cited in Theorem 2.9.
The main theorem approaches the PFR conjecture ($\varepsilon \to 0$) without reaching $\varepsilon = 0$.
